title: [$250] Perf: defer personal details weighting in SuggestionMention

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Context Dedicated tracking issue for [#88332](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88332). ## Problem On accounts with a large number of personal details (~17k), opening a report (especially invoice reports) spent significant time inside `SuggestionMention` computing `weightedPersonalDetails` via a `useMemo`. That memo ran on every render and spread-copied all personal details (`{...detail, weight}`) even when the user was not typing a mention. ## Approach (PR) - Defer weighting until a mention is actually being resolved - Remove precomputing `weightedPersonalDetails` at render time - Move `getPersonalDetailsWeight` and weighted-details computation into `getUserMentionOptions`, which only runs inside the debounced `calculateMentionSuggestion` when `prefixType === '@'` and `isMentionCode` passes - `getUserMentionOptions` takes only `searchValue` instead of a pre-built weighted map No UI change; same inputs → same outputs. O(large contact list) allocation cost moves off the render path onto the debounced keystroke path. ## Related - PR: [#88332](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88332) - Previously linked from PR: [#77173](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/77173), [#80560](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/80560)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab

  • Upwork Job ID: 2046240215213990549

title: [$250] Implement stable two-section chat switcher results (local + server)

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Context Design exploration and mocks: [Explore UX for chat switcher results that shift when backend data loads](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/83626). **Figma:** [NewDot Improvements (chat switcher — two sections)](https://www.figma.com/design/DydNEzU8NjWjqA0WXWSCJF/NewDot-Improvements?node-id=1836-642703) ## Problem The chat switcher (Cmd+K / search) shows **local/cached matches immediately**, then **rebuilds and reorders** the list when **`SearchForReports`** (or equivalent) returns. Users often open the **wrong** chat because rows move under the cursor or keyboard highlight while they are scanning or about to select. ## Agreed UX (from design) Use **two fixed sections** with **section headers** (not one merged list that re-sorts when the API returns): | Section | Source | Behavior | |--------|--------|----------| | **Top** (e.g. Recents) | Offline / cached / local | Renders **immediately**. **Order must not change** when server data arrives. | | **Bottom** (e.g. Search results) | Server / API | Populates **after** the request completes. Reordering is **only within this section**. | **Rationale:** A single “wait until online API returns” list was considered fragile because **connectivity is often flaky**; partitioned sections stay predictable across network conditions. **Out of scope for this discussion:** Reordering the list “around” the focused row without moving that row — rejected as hacky and unpredictable. **Loading UI for the bottom section** (spinner vs placeholders vs append on each query change) should match Figma and avoid duplicate or jarring spinners — confirm with design during implementation if the mock leaves ambiguity. ## Acceptance criteria 1. Typing in the chat switcher shows the **top section** from local/cached data **without** that section’s rows **reordering** when the bottom section loads or updates. 2. The **bottom section** shows server-backed results (or empty / loading per Figma); **only** this section may reorder when new API data arrives. 3. **Keyboard and pointer** selection stay aligned with **visible row identity** (no accidental navigation to a different chat because the list shifted under the user). 4. **Edge cases** covered or explicitly documented: no local matches; only local matches; slow API; offline / poor network; rapid query changes (debounce + bottom-section loading behavior). ## Follow-up (optional / separate issues) The same “local first, server second” pattern exists in other surfaces (e.g. mentions). This issue targets the **chat switcher** first; a reusable pattern can be documented or spun out after this ships. ## Design issue This work implements the UX agreed in [#83626](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/83626). Close or link that issue per team process once this is filed and triaged.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abdulrahuman5196
Upwork Automation - Do Not Edit
  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022046240993293880273
  • Upwork Job ID: 2046240993293880273
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-04-20

Launch https://staging.new.expensify.com and login with a gmail account 2. Have a tag on your Workspace (or create a WS and add a tag to it) 3. Create an expense on the Workspace 4. Create an IOU to another user 5. Go to Reports and select both the WS and the IOU expenses 6. Click on the x selected dropdown and Edit multiple ## Expected Result: Tag is not shown among the options since it can't be added/edited to all the expenses. This is how all such features usually work as for example you can't unhold two expenses when not all of them are held ## Actual Result: The tag is shown even though it can't be applied to the IOU expense ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2efe5cba-1036-4b55-8d4c-1b73a9e5fc22 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] HybridApp - Switch to Expensify Classic silently does nothing on mobile when NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT is populated before the SESSION callback fires

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
**Version Number:** v9.3.60-17 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Likely (introduced in [#87101](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87101), deployed 2026-04-08) **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** jules@expensify.com **Issue reported by:** @Julesssss (noticed while verifying [#88182](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/88182) fix) ## Action Performed 1. Open the NewDot app on HybridApp (iOS or Android Native) 2. Do not start a GPS trip 3. Navigate to Account -> Troubleshoot 4. Tap "Switch to Expensify Classic" ## Expected Result App hands off to Expensify Classic. `NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT.classicRedirect.dismissed` flips to `true` server-side. ## Actual Result Nothing happens. User stays in NewDot. `NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT` is unchanged server-side: ```json { "classicRedirect": { "dismissed": false, "timestamp": "2026-04-07T15:20:32.352Z" }, "distance": { "enabled": "true" }, "smartScan": { "enabled": "true" }, "tappedTryNewExpensifyButton": { "timestamp": "2026-04-07T15:20:32.352Z" } } ``` Web is unaffected (web does not call `closeReactNativeApp` for this flow). ## Root Cause In [`src/libs/actions/HybridApp/index.ts:54-67`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/actions/HybridApp/index.ts#L54-L67), the SESSION callback unconditionally resets module-level `currentTryNewDot = undefined` and `isLoadingTryNewDot = true` on the first transition from `undefined` to the user's `accountID`: ```ts Onyx.connectWithoutView({ key: ONYXKEYS.SESSION, callback: (session) => { const nextSessionAccountID = getSessionAccountID(session); if (nextSessionAccountID === currentSessionAccountID) return; currentSessionAccountID = nextSessionAccountID; currentTryNewDot = undefined; hasReceivedTryNewDotUpdate = false; isLoadingTryNewDot = nextSessionAccountID !== undefined || isLoadingApp !== false; }, }); ``` If the NVP_TRY_NEW_DOT callback fires **before** the SESSION callback on app start (common on mobile), `currentTryNewDot` is set, then immediately blanked. Because the Onyx NVP value itself does not change, the NVP callback never re-fires to repopulate it. The module is permanently stuck with `isLoadingTryNewDot = true`. Any subsequent call to `closeReactNativeApp({shouldSetNVP: true})` hits the loading-state branch of `shouldBlockOldAppExit` at [`index.ts:87`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/actions/HybridApp/index.ts#L87) and silently returns. The Troubleshoot button is still rendered because the component-level `useOnyx` sees the NVP value fine — this is the desync. Introduced by [#87101](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87101) (merged 2026-04-08). ## Proposed Fix Skip the module-level reset on the initial `undefined -> accountID` transition. Only blank `currentTryNewDot` when the user actually changes (logout/login or cross-user switch): ```ts if (nextSessionAccountID === currentSessionAccountID) return; const isInitialSessionLoad = currentSessionAccountID === undefined; currentSessionAccountID = nextSessionAccountID; if (isInitialSessionLoad) return; currentTryNewDot = undefined; hasReceivedTryNewDotUpdate = false; isLoadingTryNewDot = nextSessionAccountID !== undefined || isLoadingApp !== false; ``` Existing tests that codify the re-block-on-session-switch behavior (`tests/unit/HybridAppActionsTest.ts:118-156`) continue to pass because they test a real accountID change, not the initial load. ## Workaround None. Users on mobile who land in this state cannot switch to Classic until they fully sign out and sign back in (which may also fail on first session). Web is unaffected. ## Platforms - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Related - [#88182](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/88182) - Deploy blocker for the GPS-specific manifestation of this silent return - [#88237](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88237) - Fix for #88182, scoped to the GPS path only [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab
